title: Stegostyla lyallii
taxonomy: Stegostyla lyallii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asclepiadaceae, however it is now more commonly classified in the family Apocynaceae. This classification may be subject to change as classification systems are constantly evolving. The genus Stegostyla is part of the larger clade of Apocynaceae and Asclepiadaceae. As part of the larger clade, Stegostyla lyallii's classification may be influenced by various factors including its phylogenetic relationships with other species. Further research may be needed to confirm the most up-to-date classification for Stegostyla lyallii.
uses: Stegostyla lyallii, commonly known as the kāmahi tree, has been used in traditional Māori medicine for its medicinal properties. It was used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bark and leaves of the tree contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. In traditional medicine, the tree's parts were used to make teas and infusions for medicinal purposes. However, further research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal benefits and safety.

parts: Stegostyla lyallii is commonly known as the New Zealand cabbage tree or ti kouka. The roots and leaves of Stegostyla lyallii are traditionally used in Maori medicine for various purposes.
